repo_prior_art.cite: eid_dml_orth_uniform_v1: fixed-overlap equivalence sanity theorem; v2 angle0: common-EIF guardrail

published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovEtAl2018ECTJ + Newey1994Econometrica: product-rate orthogonal-score validity and common first-order semiparametric variance

repo_prior_art.cite: Closest repo comparators are eid_dml_orth_uniform_v1 weak-overlap studentization frontier and v2 angle1 LRO-normalization frontier; neither states a joint pseudo-truth compatibility partition

published_prior_art.cite: ChernozhukovEtAl2018ECTJ, ChernozhukovEscancianoIchimuraNeweyRobins2022LRO, ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022ADML, VanDerLaanLuedtkeCarone2024CDML, DukesVansteelandtWhitney2024JMLR provide sufficient validity, LRO/ADML constructions, calibration, or irregularity warnings, not a three-report iff partition

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_dml_orth_uniform_v1 and v2 angle1 already use finite-support witnesses for DML/LRO/ADML separation, but not the identical-marginal-rate non-rectangular pseudo-truth witness

published_prior_art.cite: AtheyImbensWager2018JRSSB: overlap and balancing geometry, not a DML/LRO/ADML pseudo-truth non-rectangularity witness

structure_flags.label: Introduction
structure_flags.one_line: The Introduction has only five required subparagraphs and is missing the separate 'How research benefits from the conclusion' paragraph.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: VanDerLaanLuedtkeCarone2024CDML is promised as an iff-frontier comparator, but §8 defines cells only for DML, LRO, and ADML, with no calibrated-DML report included in the statement.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey misses BonviniKennedyDukesBalakrishnan2024, a closer ATE paper on doubly robust asymptotic linearity with one nuisance inconsistent or slow; it does not subsume the three-report partition but must be compared.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The locked LRO report says 'one explicit scalar moment/Jacobian/sandwich normalization' but never gives the actual scalar moment, Jacobian, or variance normalization, so the drift objects R_m, l_m, and T_m are not determinate.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The observable compatibility cone C(P0) is underdefined: the proposal does not specify primitive learner or pseudo-truth restrictions tying h_g to h_a, so the claimed non-Cartesian cone is not determined by the locked observed law.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add the missing Introduction subparagraph explicitly explaining how research benefits from the conclusion.

Pin the exact DML, LRO, ADML, and any calibrated-DML report formulas in §6/§7 before defining the drift matrices, especially the LRO scalar moment, Jacobian, sandwich normalization, and variance report.
Either add calibrated DML as a fourth report in Conjecture 1 or downgrade the abstract/related-work promise about VanDerLaanLuedtkeCarone2024CDML to a consumer/comparator rather than an iff frontier.

Add Bonvini, Kennedy, Dukes, and Balakrishnan 2024, and optionally Zheng, Bonvini, and Guo 2025, to the related-work comparison as adjacent one-good-nuisance/slow-rate inference results that do not classify DML/LRO/ADML report cells.

recommended_next_step: Revise the proposal by pinning report maps and the compatibility cone, repairing the CDML comparator promise, and adding the missing introduction paragraph and missed one-good-nuisance literature.
